heading: Responsibility for contested logistics posture management
label: 2229b Responsibility for contested logistics posture management
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this section, referred to in subsec. (d), is the date of enactment of , which was approved on .Pub. L. 119–60Dec. 18, 2025
Prior ProvisionsA prior section 2229b was renumbered .section 3072 of this title

children.text: Identifying vulnerabilities and risks across the Department of Defense enterprise for the core logistics capabilities of supply, maintenance operations, prepositioned stocks, deployment and distribution, health services support, engineering, logistics services, and operational service contracts.
Developing and executing a strategy to mitigate the vulnerabilities and risks identified under paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: the locations of sites outside the continental United States at which stocks of supplies and equipment are stored as well as the composition of those stocks;

children.label: B
children.text: the provision of adequate intra-theater sea and air capability to move material and personnel throughout the theater; and

children.label: C
children.text: the monitoring and coordination of resourcing decisions by the military departments in support of operational plans and contingencies.

children.heading: .—Deputy Management Action Group Meetings
children.label: b .—Deputy Management Action Group Meetings
children.text: In carrying out the responsibilities under subsection (a)(1) and (2), the Deputy Secretary of Defense and the Vice Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff shall co-chair at least two Deputy Management Action Group meetings each year, which shall be focused on contested logistics management and investment.

The Deputy Secretary of Defense, the Vice Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and the Commander of the United States Transportation Command, in coordination with any other Department official identified by the Secretary, shall develop and implement a strategy for carrying out the responsibilities described in subsection (a).

children.label: i
children.text: the locations of sites outside the continental United States at which stocks of supplies and equipment are prepositioned as of the date of the strategy;

children.label: ii
children.text: the status and disposition of such prepositioned stocks; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the operational or contingency plan such stocks are intended to support.

children.label: i
children.text: any shortcomings associated with the sites and prepositioned stocks described in subparagraph (A) that must be addressed to optimally execute operational and contingency plans; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any additional sites, infrastructure, or equipment that may be needed to address such shortcomings and support such plans.

children.label: i
children.text: to address the shortcomings identified under subparagraph (B)(i); and

children.label: ii
children.text: to provide for the additional sites, infrastructure, and equipment identified under subparagraph (B)(ii).

children.label: D
children.text: A prioritized list of investment recommendations for each item described in subparagraph (C).

children.label: E
children.text: An identification of each case in which the military department concerned lacks the authority or ability to access a location outside the United States for purposes of providing logistics support as required under operational and contingency plans, set forth separately by location.

children.label: F
children.text: An assessment of any existing and projected threats to sites outside the continental United States that are expected to support such operational and contingency plans.
The strategy required under paragraph (1) shall cover the period of two years following the date of the strategy and shall be updated on an biennial basis.

children.heading: .—Quarterly Briefings
children.label: d .—Quarterly Briefings
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of the enactment of this section, and quarterly thereafter until , the Deputy Secretary of Defense, the Vice Chair of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, and the Commander of the United States Transportation Command, or their representatives, shall provide to the Committees on Armed Services of the Senate and House of Representatives a briefing on the execution of the responsibilities under subsection (a)(1) and (2), including updates on the development and implementation of the strategy required under subsection (c).December 31, 2031

children.heading: .—Budget Justification Materials
children.label: e .—Budget Justification Materials
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall include in the budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the budget of the President for a fiscal year pursuant to , United States Code, a cumulative accounting of contested logistics investments represented in such budget and how such investments relate to the duties and responsibilities under subsection (a)(1) and (2).section 1105(a) of title 31
